---
title: "Identifying which features are describing isoform-specific behaviours"
author: "Peter Kjer Hansen"
date: "2025-04-01"
output:
  html_document:
    fig_width: 12
    fig_height: 5
---

# Introduction
Please set working directory to correct folders AND set thresholds for variable importance
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(dev = "svg")

#packages
library(tidyverse)
library(caret)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(broom.mixed)
library(broom)
library(readxl)
library(ggpmisc) #For easy plots of coefficients on plots
library(lsr) #Contains cohensD() function
library(nnet) #Feedforward neural network
library(UpSetR) #Package for doing upset plots
library(ggrepel) #Pacakge for making repel text
library(scales) # Default ggplot colours

#Working directory
#Exon description dataset
Path_to_exon_description_sheet <- 'INSERT PATH TO SHEET WITH EXON DESCRIPTIONS'
File_with_exon_description_sheet <- 'INSERT FILE NAME OF .XLSX WITH EXON DESCRIPTIONS'
Event_sheet <- 'INSERT SHEET NAME'
LUMIER_sheet <- 'INSERT SHEET NAME'

# Model folders
#Postrans
Postransfected_model_folder <- 'INSERT PATH TO FOLDER WITH CLASSIFIERS TRAINED ON TRANSFECTED CELLS'
#Negtrans 10 percentile with rep aware sampling, No Golgin
Negtransfected_model_folder <- 'INSERT PATH TO FOLDER WITH CLASSIFIERS TRAINED ON NON-TRANSFECTED CELLS'

# Cell datasets and summaries
Cell_data_folder <- 'INSERT PATH TO FOLDER WITH .CSVS CONTAINING THE SINGLE CELL MEASUREMENTS'

# Bootstrap confidence threshold
bootstrap_confidence_threshold <- 0.99

### Thresholds for important variables
# Threshold for the contribution ratio (pos_trans / neg_trans). Higher is more stringent
ratio_threshold <- 2.6
# Threshold for the contribution ratio X contribution prop (positive) variable. Higher is more stringent
ratio_times_importance_threshold <- 1.5

#
transfection_threshold <- 0.00065
```

```{r ISoform pair descriptions, echo = FALSE}
# Replications of sampling + classifier training + assessment
#Pairs to be test for the various sets
#These are the actual isoform tests
PAIRS_1A <- c('PAIR_01','PAIR_02','PAIR_03','PAIR_04','PAIR_05','PAIR_06','PAIR_07','PAIR_08','PAIR_09','PAIR_10', 'PAIR_PosControl', 'PAIR_RAB5A')
PAIRS_1A_alternative_comps <- c('PAIR_03DExVSPanEx','PAIR_03bothEx','PAIR_03ExVsEx','PAIR_03Ex1VsBoth')
PAIRS_B <- c('PAIR_01','PAIR_02','PAIR_03','PAIR_04','PAIR_05','PAIR_06','PAIR_07','PAIR_08','PAIR_09','PAIR_10')
PAIRS_C <- c('PAIR_02','PAIR_04','PAIR_05','PAIR_06','PAIR_07','PAIR_08','PAIR_09','PAIR_10')
#
PAIRS_D_wo_01 <- c('PAIR_02','PAIR_05','PAIR_08','PAIR_09')
PAIRS_D_alternative_comps <- c('PAIR_01wtVS57','PAIR_01wtVS168','PAIR_01dex57VS168','PAIR_08DExVSaltEx','PAIR_08bothEx','PAIR_08ExVsEx','PAIR_08Ex1VsBoth')

```

```{r Function to load in the classifier performance and importance, echo=FALSE}
#
Replicate_performance_and_importance <- function(MODEL_FOLDER, SET, PAIR, TRANSFECTION_STATUS, PERCENTILE_CUTOFF = 0){
  if(TRANSFECTION_STATUS == 'POSITIVE'){
    load(paste0(MODEL_FOLDER,'/',SET,'_',PAIR))
  }
  if(TRANSFECTION_STATUS == 'NEGATIVE'){
    load(paste0(MODEL_FOLDER,'/NegTransModel_',SET,'_',PAIR))
  }
  #
  modeL_fit_and_tune <- function(variable){
    a <- variable$results %>%
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(ROC)) %>% 
      dplyr::arrange(desc(ROC)) %>% 
      dplyr::slice_head(n = 1) %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(size_decay_tune = paste0('size:',size,'_decay:',decay))
    return(a)
  }
  #
  variable_importance_ranking <- function(variable){
    variable_importance <- variable$variable_importance %>% 
      arrange(desc(Overall)) %>%
      rownames_to_column(var = 'Feature') %>% 
      as_tibble()
    
    rank <- seq(1, dim(variable_importance)[1])
    
    variable_importance <- bind_cols(variable_importance, 'Rank' = rank)
    return(variable_importance)
  }
  #
  n_cells <- function(variable){
    cell_count <- variable$training_data_intensity %>% 
    count()
    return(cell_count)
  }
  #
  
  ISOFORM_MODELS <- as.list(replicate_nnet[1,]) #Converts matrix rows into lists
  NEGATIVE_MODELS <- as.list(replicate_nnet[2,])
  POSITIVE_MODELS <- as.list(replicate_nnet[3,])
  
  #
  isoform_model_fit_and_tune <- lapply(ISOFORM_MODELS, FUN = modeL_fit_and_tune)
  isoform_variable_importance <- lapply(ISOFORM_MODELS, FUN = variable_importance_ranking)
  isoform_n_cells <- lapply(ISOFORM_MODELS, FUN = n_cells)
  #
  negative_fit_and_tune <- lapply(NEGATIVE_MODELS, FUN = modeL_fit_and_tune)
  negative_variable_importance <- lapply(NEGATIVE_MODELS, FUN = variable_importance_ranking)
  negative_n_cells <- lapply(NEGATIVE_MODELS, FUN = n_cells)
  #
  positive_fit_and_tune <- lapply(POSITIVE_MODELS, FUN = modeL_fit_and_tune)
  positive_variable_importance <- lapply(POSITIVE_MODELS, FUN = variable_importance_ranking)
  positive_n_cells <- lapply(POSITIVE_MODELS, FUN = n_cells)
  
  #
  iso_performance <- bind_rows(isoform_model_fit_and_tune, .id = 'Replicate')
  neg_performance <- bind_rows(negative_fit_and_tune, .id = 'Replicate')
  pos_performance <- bind_rows(positive_fit_and_tune, .id = 'Replicate') %>% 
    rename_with(~paste0(.,"_pos"), -Replicate)
  
  #
  iso_n <- bind_rows(isoform_n_cells, .id = 'Replicate')
  neg_n <- bind_rows(negative_n_cells, .id = 'Replicate')
  pos_n <- bind_rows(positive_n_cells, .id = 'Replicate')
  n_cells_all_models <- bind_rows(iso_n, neg_n, pos_n, .id = 'Group')%>% 
    dplyr::mutate('Group' = as_factor(case_when(Group == 1 ~ 'Isoform',
                                                Group == 2 ~ 'Negative',
                                                Group == 3 ~ 'Positive')),
                  'Group' = fct_relevel(Group, 'Negative','Isoform', 'Positive'))
  
  ###
  pair_x_performance <- bind_rows(iso_performance, neg_performance, bind_rows(positive_fit_and_tune, .id = 'Replicate'),.id = 'Group') %>% 
    dplyr::mutate('Group' = as_factor(case_when(Group == 1 ~ 'Isoform',
                                                Group == 2 ~ 'Negative',
                                                Group == 3 ~ 'Positive')),
                  'Group' = fct_relevel(Group, 'Negative','Isoform', 'Positive'))
  
  #
  
  ###
  relative_performance <- full_join(x = iso_performance, y = neg_performance, by = c('Replicate'), suffix = c('_iso', '_neg')) %>% 
    full_join(x = ., y = pos_performance, by = c('Replicate')) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate('ROC_difference_from_neg' = ROC_iso - ROC_neg,
                  'ROC_difference_from_pos' = ROC_iso - ROC_pos,
                  'ROC_difference_pos_from_neg' = ROC_pos - ROC_neg)
  
  #
  
  ###
  number_of_classifier_iterations <- length(isoform_variable_importance)
  number_of_features <- length(unique(isoform_variable_importance[[1]]$Feature))
  #
  iso_importance <- bind_rows(isoform_variable_importance, .id = 'Replicate') %>% 
    ungroup() %>% 
    group_by(Feature) %>% 
    summarize('Rank_sum' = sum(Rank),
              'Rank_median' = median(Rank),
              'Rank_05percent' = quantile(Rank, probs = c(0.05)),
              'Rank_10percent' = quantile(Rank, probs = c(0.1)),
              'Rank_25percent' = quantile(Rank, probs = c(0.25)),
              'Rank_75percent' = quantile(Rank, probs = c(0.75)),
              'Rank_90percent' = quantile(Rank, probs = c(0.9)),
              'Rank_95percent' = quantile(Rank, probs = c(0.95)),
              'Overall_sum' = sum(Overall),
              'Overall_sd' = sd(Overall)) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate('No_iterations' = number_of_classifier_iterations,
                  'Highest_potential_overall' = 100 * number_of_classifier_iterations,
                  'Highest_potential_rank' = number_of_classifier_iterations,
                  'Lowest_potential_rank' = number_of_classifier_iterations * number_of_features,
                  'Rank_75_25' = Rank_75percent - Rank_25percent,
                  'Rank_90_10' = Rank_90percent - Rank_10percent,
                  'Rank_95_05' = Rank_95percent - Rank_05percent) %>%
    dplyr::mutate('Rank_avg' = Rank_sum/No_iterations,
                  'Overall_avg' = Overall_sum/No_iterations) %>% 
    dplyr::arrange(Rank_sum)
  #
  neg_importance <- bind_rows(negative_variable_importance, .id = 'Replicate') %>% 
    ungroup() %>% 
    group_by(Feature) %>% 
    summarize('Rank_sum' = sum(Rank),
              'Rank_median' = median(Rank),
              'Rank_05percent' = quantile(Rank, probs = c(0.05)),
              'Rank_10percent' = quantile(Rank, probs = c(0.1)),
              'Rank_25percent' = quantile(Rank, probs = c(0.25)),
              'Rank_75percent' = quantile(Rank, probs = c(0.75)),
              'Rank_90percent' = quantile(Rank, probs = c(0.9)),
              'Rank_95percent' = quantile(Rank, probs = c(0.95)),
              'Overall_sum' = sum(Overall),
              'Overall_sd' = sd(Overall)) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate('No_iterations' = number_of_classifier_iterations,
                  'Highest_potential_overall' = 100 * number_of_classifier_iterations,
                  'Highest_potential_rank' = number_of_classifier_iterations,
                  'Lowest_potential_rank' = number_of_classifier_iterations * number_of_features,
                  'Rank_75_25' = Rank_75percent - Rank_25percent,
                  'Rank_90_10' = Rank_90percent - Rank_10percent,
                  'Rank_95_05' = Rank_95percent - Rank_05percent) %>%
    dplyr::mutate('Rank_avg' = Rank_sum/No_iterations,
                  'Overall_avg' = Overall_sum/No_iterations) %>% 
    dplyr::arrange(Rank_sum)
  #
  neg_importance_reduced <- neg_importance %>% 
    dplyr::select(Feature, Rank_sum, Rank_avg, Rank_median, Rank_90_10, Rank_95_05, Overall_sum, Overall_avg, Overall_sd)
  
    #
  iso_neg_comb_importance <- dplyr::full_join(x = iso_importance, y = neg_importance_reduced, by = c('Feature'), suffix = c('','_neg')) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate('Rank_median_difference' = Rank_median_neg - Rank_median,
                  'Rank_sum_difference' = Rank_sum_neg - Rank_sum,
                  'Rank_90_10_difference' = Rank_90_10_neg - Rank_90_10,
                  'Rank_95_05_difference' = Rank_95_05_neg - Rank_95_05)
  
  
  #
  
  results_list <- list('performance' = pair_x_performance, 'SET' = SET, 'PAIR' = PAIR, 'relative_performance' = relative_performance, 'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = TRANSFECTION_STATUS, 'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = PERCENTILE_CUTOFF,
                       'var_importance_iso' = iso_importance, 'var_importance_neg' = negative_variable_importance, 'var_importance_pos' = positive_variable_importance,
                       'var_importance_iso_w_contrast' = iso_neg_comb_importance, 'n_cells' = n_cells_all_models)
  return(results_list)
}
```

```{r Loading Positive transfectant models, echo = FALSE}
#Reading in all the models (For 1, A, 1A, B, C, and E)
#Positive transfectant models
#####

#Set 1A
for(i in PAIRS_1A){
  test <- Replicate_performance_and_importance(MODEL_FOLDER = Postransfected_model_folder,
                                               SET = 'SET_1A',
                                               PAIR = i,
                                               TRANSFECTION_STATUS = 'POSITIVE')
  #
  
  #Creates absolute performance metrics (single metrics)
  assign(paste0('postrans_model_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR),test$performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF) %>% 
           left_join(., y = test$n_cells, by = c('Replicate','Group')))
  #Creates relative performance object
  assign(paste0('postrans_relative_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$relative_performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
  #Creates variable importance object
  assign(paste0('postrans_iso_variable_contrast_importance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$var_importance_iso_w_contrast %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
}
#Set 1A alternative comparisons
for(i in PAIRS_1A_alternative_comps){
  test <- Replicate_performance_and_importance(MODEL_FOLDER = Postransfected_model_folder,
                                               SET = 'SET_1A',
                                               PAIR = i,
                                               TRANSFECTION_STATUS = 'POSITIVE')

  #Creates absolute performance metrics (single metrics)
  assign(paste0('postrans_model_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR),test$performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF) %>% 
           left_join(., y = test$n_cells, by = c('Replicate','Group')))
  #Creates relative performance object
  assign(paste0('postrans_relative_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$relative_performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
  #Creates variable importance object
  assign(paste0('postrans_iso_variable_contrast_importance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$var_importance_iso_w_contrast %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
}
#Set B
for(i in PAIRS_B){
  test <- Replicate_performance_and_importance(MODEL_FOLDER = Postransfected_model_folder,
                                               SET = 'SET_B',
                                               PAIR = i,
                                               TRANSFECTION_STATUS = 'POSITIVE')
  
  #Creates absolute performance metrics (single metrics)
  assign(paste0('postrans_model_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR),test$performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF) %>% 
           left_join(., y = test$n_cells, by = c('Replicate','Group')))
  #Creates relative performance object
  assign(paste0('postrans_relative_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$relative_performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
  #Creates variable importance object
  assign(paste0('postrans_iso_variable_contrast_importance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$var_importance_iso_w_contrast %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
}
#Set C
for(i in PAIRS_C){
  test <- Replicate_performance_and_importance(MODEL_FOLDER = Postransfected_model_folder,
                                               SET = 'SET_C',
                                               PAIR = i,
                                               TRANSFECTION_STATUS = 'POSITIVE')
  
  #Creates absolute performance metrics (single metrics)
  assign(paste0('postrans_model_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR),test$performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF) %>% 
           left_join(., y = test$n_cells, by = c('Replicate','Group')))
  #Creates relative performance object
  assign(paste0('postrans_relative_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$relative_performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
  #Creates variable importance object
  assign(paste0('postrans_iso_variable_contrast_importance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$var_importance_iso_w_contrast %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
}
#Set D
for(i in PAIRS_D_wo_01){
  test <- Replicate_performance_and_importance(MODEL_FOLDER = Postransfected_model_folder,
                                               SET = 'SET_D',
                                               PAIR = i,
                                               TRANSFECTION_STATUS = 'POSITIVE')
  
  #Creates absolute performance metrics (single metrics)
  assign(paste0('postrans_model_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR),test$performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF) %>% 
           left_join(., y = test$n_cells, by = c('Replicate','Group')))
  #Creates relative performance object
  assign(paste0('postrans_relative_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$relative_performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
  #Creates variable importance object
  assign(paste0('postrans_iso_variable_contrast_importance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$var_importance_iso_w_contrast %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
}
#Set D, alternative comparisons
for(i in PAIRS_D_alternative_comps){
  test <- Replicate_performance_and_importance(MODEL_FOLDER = Postransfected_model_folder,
                                               SET = 'SET_D',
                                               PAIR = i,
                                               TRANSFECTION_STATUS = 'POSITIVE')
  
  #Creates absolute performance metrics (single metrics)
  assign(paste0('postrans_model_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR),test$performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF) %>% 
           left_join(., y = test$n_cells, by = c('Replicate','Group')))
  #Creates relative performance object
  assign(paste0('postrans_relative_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$relative_performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
  #Creates variable importance object
  assign(paste0('postrans_iso_variable_contrast_importance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$var_importance_iso_w_contrast %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
}
```

```{r Loading negative transfectant models 10 percentile, echo = FALSE}
#Reading in all the models (For 1, A, 1A, B, C, and E)
#Negative transfectant models
#####

#Set 1A
for(i in PAIRS_1A){
  test <- Replicate_performance_and_importance(MODEL_FOLDER = Negtransfected_model_folder,
                                               SET = 'SET_1A',
                                               PAIR = i,
                                               TRANSFECTION_STATUS = 'NEGATIVE',
                                               PERCENTILE_CUTOFF = 10)
  #
  
  #Creates absolute performance metrics (single metrics)
  assign(paste0('negtrans_model_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR),test$performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF) %>% 
           left_join(., y = test$n_cells, by = c('Replicate','Group')))
  #Creates relative performance object
  assign(paste0('negtrans_relative_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$relative_performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
  #Creates variable importance object
  assign(paste0('negtrans_iso_variable_contrast_importance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$var_importance_iso_w_contrast %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
}
#Set 1A alternative comparisons
for(i in PAIRS_1A_alternative_comps){
  test <- Replicate_performance_and_importance(MODEL_FOLDER = Negtransfected_model_folder,
                                               SET = 'SET_1A',
                                               PAIR = i,
                                               TRANSFECTION_STATUS = 'NEGATIVE',
                                               PERCENTILE_CUTOFF = 10)

  #Creates absolute performance metrics (single metrics)
  assign(paste0('negtrans_model_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR),test$performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF) %>% 
           left_join(., y = test$n_cells, by = c('Replicate','Group')))
  #Creates relative performance object
  assign(paste0('negtrans_relative_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$relative_performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
  #Creates variable importance object
  assign(paste0('negtrans_iso_variable_contrast_importance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$var_importance_iso_w_contrast %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
}
#Set B
for(i in PAIRS_B){
  test <- Replicate_performance_and_importance(MODEL_FOLDER = Negtransfected_model_folder,
                                               SET = 'SET_B',
                                               PAIR = i,
                                               TRANSFECTION_STATUS = 'NEGATIVE',
                                               PERCENTILE_CUTOFF = 10)
  
  #Creates absolute performance metrics (single metrics)
  assign(paste0('negtrans_model_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR),test$performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF) %>% 
           left_join(., y = test$n_cells, by = c('Replicate','Group')))
  #Creates relative performance object
  assign(paste0('negtrans_relative_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$relative_performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
  #Creates variable importance object
  assign(paste0('negtrans_iso_variable_contrast_importance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$var_importance_iso_w_contrast %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
}
#Set C
for(i in PAIRS_C){
  test <- Replicate_performance_and_importance(MODEL_FOLDER = Negtransfected_model_folder,
                                               SET = 'SET_C',
                                               PAIR = i,
                                               TRANSFECTION_STATUS = 'NEGATIVE',
                                               PERCENTILE_CUTOFF = 10)
  
  #Creates absolute performance metrics (single metrics)
  assign(paste0('negtrans_model_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR),test$performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF) %>% 
           left_join(., y = test$n_cells, by = c('Replicate','Group')))
  #Creates relative performance object
  assign(paste0('negtrans_relative_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$relative_performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
  #Creates variable importance object
  assign(paste0('negtrans_iso_variable_contrast_importance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$var_importance_iso_w_contrast %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
}
#
for(i in PAIRS_D_wo_01){
  test <- Replicate_performance_and_importance(MODEL_FOLDER = Negtransfected_model_folder,
                                               SET = 'SET_D',
                                               PAIR = i,
                                               TRANSFECTION_STATUS = 'NEGATIVE',
                                               PERCENTILE_CUTOFF = 10)
  
  #Creates absolute performance metrics (single metrics)
  assign(paste0('negtrans_model_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR),test$performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF) %>% 
           left_join(., y = test$n_cells, by = c('Replicate','Group')))
  #Creates relative performance object
  assign(paste0('negtrans_relative_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$relative_performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
  #Creates variable importance object
  assign(paste0('negtrans_iso_variable_contrast_importance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$var_importance_iso_w_contrast %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
}
#SET D, ALTERNATIVE COMPARISONS
for(i in PAIRS_D_alternative_comps){
  test <- Replicate_performance_and_importance(MODEL_FOLDER = Negtransfected_model_folder,
                                               SET = 'SET_D',
                                               PAIR = i,
                                               TRANSFECTION_STATUS = 'NEGATIVE',
                                               PERCENTILE_CUTOFF = 10)
  
  #Creates absolute performance metrics (single metrics)
  assign(paste0('negtrans_model_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR),test$performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF) %>% 
           left_join(., y = test$n_cells, by = c('Replicate','Group')))
  #Creates relative performance object
  assign(paste0('negtrans_relative_performance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$relative_performance %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
  #Creates variable importance object
  assign(paste0('negtrans_iso_variable_contrast_importance_',test$SET,'_',test$PAIR), test$var_importance_iso_w_contrast %>% 
           dplyr::mutate('SET' = test$SET,
                         'ISOFORM_PAIR' = test$PAIR,
                         'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' = test$TRANSFECTION_STATUS,
                         'PERCENTILE_CUTOFF' = test$PERCENTILE_CUTOFF))
}

```

```{r Loading exon descriptions, echo = FALSE}
#Exon description
Exon_descriptions <- readxl::read_xlsx(path = paste0(Path_to_exon_description_sheet, File_with_exon_description_sheet),
                                       sheet = Event_sheet) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate('microexon' = Exon_length_NT <= 30) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(Gene_name_Rclassifier = case_when( #Note, currently because names in SET D are marked w. asteriks, they have to be so in this exon description file. But excel didn't like starting the name w. an asteriks.
    Gene_name_Rclassifier == 'ATXN2' ~ '*ATXN2', #Only done for the isoform pairs that are not recurring
    Gene_name_Rclassifier == 'EVI5' ~ '*EVI5',
    Gene_name_Rclassifier == 'PPFIA1' ~ '*PPFIA1',
    Gene_name_Rclassifier == 'CLASP1' & Exon_name_Rclassifier == 'ExA' ~ 'CLASP1_ExA_vs_ExB',
    Gene_name_Rclassifier == 'CLASP1' & Exon_name_Rclassifier == 'ExB' ~ 'CLASP1_noEx_vs_ExB',
    Gene_name_Rclassifier == 'CLASP1' & Exon_name_Rclassifier == 'ExA' ~ 'CLASP1_ExA_vs_ExB',
    Gene_name_Rclassifier == 'CLASP1' & Exon_name_Rclassifier == 'ExB' ~ 'CLASP1_noEx_vs_ExB',
    .default = Gene_name_Rclassifier))

Exon_descriptions_reduced <- Exon_descriptions %>% 
  dplyr::select(microexon, Splice_event_enriched_in_neural_tissue, Splice_event_enriched_in_pancreatic_islet_cells, Enrichment_comment, Exon_length_NT, Gene_name_Rclassifier, Exon_name_Rclassifier, EVENT_ID_vastDB, Location_in_protein) #%>% 
  #dplyr::filter(!c(Gene_name_Rclassifier == 'VAV2')) #Current_naming_doesn't_work_well_with_genes_with_multiple_exons

### ISOFORM_PAIRS_EXCLUDE
ISOFORM_PAIRS_TO_EXCLUDE_DUE_TO_DOUBLES <- c('VAV2','*SH3KBP1_D/E','*OCRL_D/E','*SH3GLB1_D/E')
ISOFORM_PAIRS_TO_EXCLUDE_DUE_TO_INTENSITY <- c('VAV2','PACSIN2') ##THIS DEFINES WHICH ISOFORM PAIRS ARE EXCLUDED.

```

```{r combining absolute classifier outputs into proper datasets, echo = FALSE}
#Combining individual isoforms into proper dataset
## Absolute performance
#Set 1A
combined_model_performance_SET_1A <- bind_rows(mget(ls(pattern = 'model_performance_SET_1A_PAIR'))) #Will contain both SET_1A normal comps and alternative CLASP1 comparisons
rm(list = ls(pattern = 'model_performance_SET_1A_PAIR'))
#Set B
combined_model_performance_SET_B <- bind_rows(mget(ls(pattern = 'model_performance_SET_B_PAIR')))
rm(list = ls(pattern = 'model_performance_SET_B_PAIR'))
#Set C
combined_model_performance_SET_C <- bind_rows(mget(ls(pattern = 'model_performance_SET_C_PAIR')))
rm(list = ls(pattern = 'model_performance_SET_C_PAIR'))
#Set D
combined_model_performance_SET_D <- bind_rows(mget(ls(pattern = 'model_performance_SET_D_PAIR')))
rm(list = ls(pattern = 'model_performance_SET_D_PAIR'))

#All
combined_model_performance_all <- bind_rows(mget(ls(pattern = 'combined_model_performance_SET_'))) %>%  #WRITE CASE_WHEN TO GIVE ALL SET + PAIR COMBINATIONS THEIR GENE NAME (COULD POTENTIALLY BE GENE + EXON ID -> ALTHOUGH COMBINATIONS GET TRICKY STILL)
  dplyr::mutate('Gene_name' = case_when(
    SET == 'SET_1' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_01' ~ 'ANK2',
    SET == 'SET_1' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_03' ~ 'CLASP1_noEx_vs_ExA',
    SET == 'SET_1' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_04' ~ 'CLASP1_ExB_vs_ExA+ExB',
    SET == 'SET_1' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_08' ~ 'NUMB',
    SET == 'SET_1' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_09' ~ 'SNX2',
    #
    SET == 'SET_A' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_01' ~ 'ANK2',
    SET == 'SET_A' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_03' ~ 'CLASP1_noEx_vs_ExA',
    SET == 'SET_A' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_04' ~ 'CLASP1_ExB_vs_ExA+ExB',
    SET == 'SET_A' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_08' ~ 'NUMB',
    SET == 'SET_A' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_09' ~ 'SNX2',
    #
    SET == 'SET_1A' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_01' ~ 'ANK2',
    SET == 'SET_1A' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_02' ~ 'Apbb1',
    SET == 'SET_1A' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_03' ~ 'CLASP1_noEx_vs_ExA', #
    SET == 'SET_1A' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_03bothEx' ~ 'CLASP1_noEx_vs_ExA+ExB',
    SET == 'SET_1A' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_03DExVSPanEx' ~ 'CLASP1_noEx_vs_ExB',
    SET == 'SET_1A' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_03Ex1VsBoth' ~ 'CLASP1_ExA_vs_ExA+ExB',
    SET == 'SET_1A' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_03ExVsEx' ~ 'CLASP1_ExA_vs_ExB',
    SET == 'SET_1A' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_04' ~ 'CLASP1_ExB_vs_ExA+ExB',
    SET == 'SET_1A' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_05' ~ 'DCTN1',
    SET == 'SET_1A' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_06' ~ 'VAV2',
    SET == 'SET_1A' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_07' ~ 'FBXW11',
    SET == 'SET_1A' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_08' ~ 'NUMB',
    SET == 'SET_1A' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_09' ~ 'SNX2',
    SET == 'SET_1A' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_10' ~ 'SPAG9',
    SET == 'SET_1A' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_RAB5A' ~ 'NegControl',
    SET == 'SET_1A' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_PosControl' ~ 'PosControl',
    #
    SET == 'SET_B' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_01' ~ 'AP1G1',
    SET == 'SET_B' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_02' ~ 'DAAM1',
    SET == 'SET_B' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_03' ~ 'DNM1L',
    SET == 'SET_B' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_04' ~ 'DYNC1LI2',
    SET == 'SET_B' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_05' ~ 'EEA1',
    SET == 'SET_B' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_06' ~ 'KIF3A',
    SET == 'SET_B' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_07' ~ 'ROBO1',
    SET == 'SET_B' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_08' ~ 'SCYL2',
    SET == 'SET_B' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_09' ~ 'SRC',
    SET == 'SET_B' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_10' ~ 'TNK2',
    #
    SET == 'SET_C' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_02' ~ 'CLTA',
    SET == 'SET_C' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_04' ~ 'DNM2',
    SET == 'SET_C' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_05' ~ 'Dnm2',
    SET == 'SET_C' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_06' ~ 'Dnm3',
    SET == 'SET_C' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_07' ~ 'OCRL',
    SET == 'SET_C' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_08' ~ 'PACSIN2',
    SET == 'SET_C' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_09' ~ 'SH3GLB1',
    SET == 'SET_C' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_10' ~ 'SH3KBP1',
    #
    SET == 'SET_D' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_01dex57VS168' ~ '*APP_noEx_vs_ExA', #Ex168nt is ExA, Ex57nt is ExB
    SET == 'SET_D' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_01wtVS168' ~ '*APP_noEx_vs_ExA+ExB',
    SET == 'SET_D' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_01wtVS57' ~ '*APP_ExA_vs_ExA+ExB',
    SET == 'SET_D' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_02' ~ '*ATXN2',
    SET == 'SET_D' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_03' ~ '*EVI5',
    SET == 'SET_D' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_04' ~ '*OCRL_D/E',
    SET == 'SET_D' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_05' ~ '*PPFIA1',
    SET == 'SET_D' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_06' ~ '*SH3GLB1_D/E',
    SET == 'SET_D' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_07' ~ '*SH3KBP1_D/E',
    SET == 'SET_D' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_08' ~ '*VAV2_noEx_vs_ExA', #ExA (is likely the downstream micro-exon), ExB is upstream micro-exon (I think)
    SET == 'SET_D' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_08bothEx' ~ '*VAV2_noEx_vs_ExA+ExB',
    SET == 'SET_D' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_08DExVSaltEx' ~ '*VAV2_noEx_vs_ExB',
    SET == 'SET_D' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_08Ex1VsBoth' ~ '*VAV2_ExA_vs_ExA+ExB',
    SET == 'SET_D' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_08ExVsEx' ~ '*VAV2_ExA_vs_ExB',
    SET == 'SET_D' & ISOFORM_PAIR == 'PAIR_09' ~ '*VAV2_ExB_vs_ExA+ExB'
  )) %>% 
  #left_join(., y = SUMMARY_of_all_sample_ratios, by = join_by('SET' == 'SET', 'ISOFORM_PAIR' == 'ISOFORM_PAIR')) %>% 
  left_join(., y = Exon_descriptions_reduced, by = join_by('Gene_name' == 'Gene_name_Rclassifier')) %>% 
  #The fusion doesn't work perfectly for genes with multiple events - the line below removes an unwanted duplication of the VAV2 data (NOTE, THIS IS WHAT GIVES RISE TO THE MANY-TO-MANY RELATIONSHIP)
  dplyr::filter(!c(SET == 'SET_1A' & Gene_name == 'VAV2' & Exon_name_Rclassifier == 'ExB')) %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(microexon = case_when( #Hacky way of making all events have microexon, yes/no label
    word(Gene_name, 1, 1, sep = fixed('_')) == '*APP' ~ FALSE,
    word(Gene_name, 1, 1, sep = fixed('_')) == '*VAV2' ~ TRUE,
    word(Gene_name, 1, 1, sep = fixed('_')) == 'CLASP1' ~ TRUE,
    .default = microexon))
#


#Dataframe w. the gene name SET + PAIR combination
gene_ids_match <- combined_model_performance_all %>% 
  dplyr::select(SET, ISOFORM_PAIR, Gene_name, microexon, Exon_length_NT, EVENT_ID_vastDB) %>%
  unique()

#Dataframe w. n used for classifier and the gene names
n_used_for_classifier <- combined_model_performance_all %>% 
  dplyr::select(n, Gene_name) %>% 
  unique() %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(Gene_name) %>% 
  summarize(n = mean(n))

```

```{r creating relative ROC that compares negative transfectant and positive transfectant ROCs, echo = FALSE}
#Splitting the combined ROC dataset according to transfection status
#Positive transfectants
combined_model_performance_all_postrans <- combined_model_performance_all %>% 
  dplyr::filter(TRANSFECTION_STATUS == 'POSITIVE')
#Negative transfectants
combined_model_performance_all_negtrans <- combined_model_performance_all %>% 
  dplyr::filter(TRANSFECTION_STATUS == 'NEGATIVE')

#Joining positive and negative transfectants in a wide-format
combined_model_performance_all_posneg_relative <- full_join(x = combined_model_performance_all_postrans, y = combined_model_performance_all_negtrans,
                                                            by = join_by(SET, ISOFORM_PAIR, Group, Replicate),
                                                            suffix = c('_postrans', '_negtrans')) %>%
  # Creating ROC relative column (pos - neg). Can be negative.
  dplyr::mutate(ROC_posneg_relative = ROC_postrans - ROC_negtrans,
                tmp_norm_ROC = (ROC_postrans - ROC_negtrans)/(1 - ROC_negtrans),
                Ceiling_normalised_ROC = if_else(tmp_norm_ROC >= 0, tmp_norm_ROC, (ROC_postrans - ROC_negtrans)/(1 - ROC_postrans)))
    
```

### Testing ceiling normalised ROC-AUC distributions for isoform classifiers using boot strap confidence intervals

```{r Function to perform bootstrap confidence interval caluclations, echo = FALSE}
#' Bootstrap Test for Pre-Normalized ROC Scores with Group Support
#'
#' This function calculates bootstrap confidence intervals for normalized ROC scores
#' that have already been calculated. It supports grouping by a variable (e.g., gene name).
#'
#' @param data A data frame containing the normalized scores
#' @param norm_col Name of the column containing normalized ROC values
#' @param group_col Name of the column for grouping (e.g., "Gene_name")
#' @param group_value Value to filter by in the group column (e.g., specific gene name)
#' @param conf_level Confidence level (default 0.95)
#' @param n_bootstrap Number of bootstrap iterations (default 2000)
#' @param one_sided Logical; if TRUE, returns one-sided interval (default TRUE)
#'
#' @return A list containing bootstrap statistics and test results
#'
#' @examples
#' # Example with pre-calculated normalized values
#' data <- data.frame(
#'   Gene_name = rep(c("Gene1", "Gene2"), each = 50),
#'   norm_roc = c(rnorm(50, 0.3, 0.1), rnorm(50, -0.1, 0.2))
#' )
#' result <- bootstrap_norm_roc_grouped(data, "norm_roc", "Gene_name", "Gene1")
#' print(result)
#'
bootstrap_norm_roc_grouped <- function(data, 
                                       norm_col = "norm_roc", 
                                       group_col = NULL,
                                       group_value = NULL,
                                       conf_level = 0.95,
                                       n_bootstrap = 2000,
                                       one_sided = TRUE) {
  
  # Require boot package
  if (!requireNamespace("boot", quietly = TRUE)) {
    stop("Please install the 'boot' package: install.packages('boot')")
  }
  
  # Filter data by group if specified
  if (!is.null(group_col) && !is.null(group_value)) {
    if (!(group_col %in% colnames(data))) {
      stop(paste("Group column", group_col, "not found in data"))
    }
    data <- data[data[[group_col]] == group_value, ]
    if (nrow(data) == 0) {
      stop(paste("No data found for", group_col, "=", group_value))
    }
  }
  
  # Check if norm_col exists
  if (!(norm_col %in% colnames(data))) {
    stop(paste("Normalized score column", norm_col, "not found in data"))
  }
  
  # Extract normalized scores
  norm_scores <- data[[norm_col]]
  
  # Function for bootstrapping
  boot_func <- function(data, indices) {
    return(mean(data[indices]))
  }
  
  # Perform bootstrap
  boot_result <- boot::boot(norm_scores, boot_func, R = n_bootstrap)
  
  # Calculate confidence interval
  if (one_sided) {
    # One-sided interval (lower bound only)
    alpha <- 1 - conf_level
    ci <- boot::boot.ci(boot_result, type = "perc", conf = conf_level)
    lower_bound <- ci$percent[4]  # Lower bound of percentile interval
    upper_bound <- Inf
  } else {
    # Two-sided interval
    ci <- boot::boot.ci(boot_result, type = "perc", conf = conf_level)
    lower_bound <- ci$percent[4]  # Lower bound
    upper_bound <- ci$percent[5]  # Upper bound
  }
  
  # Determine if zero is in the confidence interval
  zero_in_ci <- (lower_bound <= 0 && upper_bound >= 0)
  
  # Create summary results
  result <- list(
    mean = mean(norm_scores),
    median = median(norm_scores),
    sd = sd(norm_scores),
    ci_lower = lower_bound,
    ci_upper = ifelse(one_sided, Inf, upper_bound),
    one_sided = one_sided,
    conf_level = conf_level,
    zero_in_ci = zero_in_ci,
    significant = !zero_in_ci,
    n_iterations = length(norm_scores),
    n_bootstrap = n_bootstrap,
    group_col = group_col,
    group_value = group_value,
    raw_scores = norm_scores,
    boot_result = boot_result
  )
  
  return(result)
}

#' Run Bootstrap Tests for Multiple Groups
#'
#' This function runs bootstrap tests for multiple groups and returns results in a data frame
#'
#' @param data A data frame containing the normalized scores
#' @param norm_col Name of the column containing normalized ROC values
#' @param group_col Name of the column for grouping (e.g., "Gene_name")
#' @param conf_level Confidence level (default 0.95)
#' @param n_bootstrap Number of bootstrap iterations (default 2000)
#' @param one_sided Logical; if TRUE, returns one-sided interval (default TRUE)
#' @param adjust_method Method for multiple testing correction (default "BH"). Options include 
#'                     "holm", "hochberg", "hommel", "bonferroni", "BH", "BY", "fdr", "none"
#'
#' @return A data frame with bootstrap test results for each group, including adjusted p-values
#'
#' @examples
#' # Example with pre-calculated normalized values for multiple genes
#' data <- data.frame(
#'   Gene_name = rep(c("Gene1", "Gene2", "Gene3"), each = 50),
#'   norm_roc = c(rnorm(50, 0.3, 0.1), rnorm(50, -0.1, 0.2), rnorm(50, 0.15, 0.15))
#' )
#' results <- bootstrap_multiple_groups(data, "norm_roc", "Gene_name")
#' print(results)
#'
bootstrap_multiple_groups <- function(data, 
                                      norm_col = "norm_roc", 
                                      group_col,
                                      conf_level = 0.95,
                                      n_bootstrap = 2000,
                                      one_sided = TRUE,
                                      adjust_method = "BH") {
  
  # Get unique groups
  groups <- unique(data[[group_col]])
  
  # Create empty results data frame
  results <- data.frame(
    group = character(),
    mean = numeric(),
    median = numeric(),
    sd = numeric(),
    ci_lower = numeric(),
    ci_upper = numeric(),
    p_value = numeric(),
    significant = logical(),
    adj_p_value = numeric(),
    adj_significant = logical(),
    n_iterations = numeric(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Loop through groups
  group_results <- list()
  p_values <- numeric(length(groups))
  
  for (i in seq_along(groups)) {
    group <- groups[i]
    # Run bootstrap test
    boot_result <- bootstrap_norm_roc_grouped(
      data = data,
      norm_col = norm_col,
      group_col = group_col,
      group_value = group,
      conf_level = conf_level,
      n_bootstrap = n_bootstrap,
      one_sided = one_sided
    )
    
    # Calculate p-value from bootstrap distribution
    if (one_sided) {
      # For one-sided test, calculate p-value as proportion of bootstrap values <= 0
      p_value <- mean(boot_result$boot_result$t <= 0)
    } else {
      # For two-sided test, calculate p-value based on whether 0 is in the CI
      # This is approximate but aligns with the confidence interval approach
      z_stat <- boot_result$mean / (boot_result$sd / sqrt(boot_result$n_iterations))
      p_value <- if (boot_result$mean >= 0) 2 * pnorm(-abs(z_stat)) else 2 * pnorm(-abs(z_stat))
    }
    
    p_values[i] <- p_value
    
    # Store result
    group_results[[i]] <- data.frame(
      group = group,
      mean = boot_result$mean,
      median = boot_result$median,
      sd = boot_result$sd,
      ci_lower = boot_result$ci_lower,
      ci_upper = ifelse(one_sided, Inf, boot_result$ci_upper),
      p_value = p_value,
      significant = boot_result$significant,
      n_iterations = boot_result$n_iterations
    )
  }
  
  # Adjust p-values for multiple testing
  adj_p_values <- p.adjust(p_values, method = adjust_method)
  
  # Combine results
  results <- do.call(rbind, group_results)
  results$adj_p_value <- adj_p_values
  results$adj_significant <- results$adj_p_value < (1 - conf_level)
  
  # Return results
  return(results)
}

#' Visualize Bootstrap Distribution
#'
#' Creates a histogram of bootstrap means with confidence interval
#'
#' @param boot_result Result from bootstrap_norm_roc_grouped function
#' @param bins Number of histogram bins (default 30)
#'
#' @return A ggplot2 object
#'
plot_bootstrap_dist <- function(boot_result, bins = 30) {
  # Require ggplot2
  if (!requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {
    stop("Please install the 'ggplot2' package: install.packages('ggplot2')")
  }
  
  # Extract bootstrap samples
  t_values <- boot_result$boot_result$t
  
  # Create dataframe for plotting
  df <- data.frame(value = t_values)
  
  # Determine title suffix based on grouping
  group_suffix <- ""
  if (!is.null(boot_result$group_col) && !is.null(boot_result$group_value)) {
    group_suffix <- paste(" for", boot_result$group_col, "=", boot_result$group_value)
  }
  
  # Create plot
  p <- ggplot2::ggplot(df, ggplot2::aes(x = value)) +
    ggplot2::geom_histogram(bins = bins, fill = "steelblue", color = "white", alpha = 0.7) +
    ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = boot_result$ci_lower, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
    ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black", linetype = "solid") +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = paste0("Bootstrap Distribution of Normalized ROC Score", group_suffix),
      subtitle = paste0(
        ifelse(boot_result$one_sided, "One-sided ", "Two-sided "), 
        boot_result$conf_level * 100, "% CI: [", 
        round(boot_result$ci_lower, 4), ", ", 
        ifelse(boot_result$one_sided, "∞", round(boot_result$ci_upper, 4)), "]"
      ),
      x = "Normalized ROC Score",
      y = "Frequency",
      caption = paste("Mean:", round(boot_result$mean, 4), 
                      "| Significant:", boot_result$significant)
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal()
  
  return(p)
}

#' Create Forest Plot of Bootstrap Results
#'
#' Creates a forest plot of bootstrap results for multiple groups
#'
#' @param results Data frame from bootstrap_multiple_groups function
#' @param sort_by Column to sort by (default "mean")
#' @param decreasing Logical; if TRUE, sorts in decreasing order (default TRUE)
#' @param color_by_significance Logical; if TRUE, colors points by significance (default TRUE)
#'
#' @return A ggplot2 object
#'
plot_forest <- function(results, 
                        sort_by = "mean", 
                        decreasing = TRUE,
                        color_by_significance = TRUE,
                        use_adjusted_significance = TRUE) {
  
  # Require ggplot2
  if (!requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {
    stop("Please install the 'ggplot2' package: install.packages('ggplot2')")
  }
  
  # Sort results
  results <- results[order(results[[sort_by]], decreasing = decreasing), ]
  
  # Make sure group is a factor in the right order for plotting
  results$group <- factor(results$group, levels = results$group)
  
  # Determine which significance column to use
  sig_col <- if(use_adjusted_significance && "adj_significant" %in% colnames(results)) {
    "adj_significant"
  } else {
    "significant"
  }
  
  # Create plot
  p <- ggplot2::ggplot(results, ggplot2::aes(x = mean, y = group)) +
    ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
    ggplot2::geom_errorbarh(ggplot2::aes(xmin = ci_lower, xmax = ci_upper), height = 0.2) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(ggplot2::aes(color = if(color_by_significance) .data[[sig_col]] else NULL), 
                        size = 3) +
    scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(levels(results$group)))+
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = paste0("Normalized ROC Scores with Bootstrap Confidence Intervals",
                     ifelse(use_adjusted_significance && "adj_significant" %in% colnames(results),
                            "\n(adjusted for multiple testing)", "")),
      x = "Normalized ROC Score",
      y = NULL,
      subtitle = ifelse("adj_p_value" %in% colnames(results), 
                        paste0("Multiple testing correction: ", 
                               ifelse(any(results$adj_p_value != results$p_value), "Applied", "None")),
                        "")
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
    ggplot2::theme(
      axis.text.y = ggplot2::element_text(size = 10),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )
  
  # Add color scale if coloring by significance
  if (color_by_significance) {
    p <- p + ggplot2::scale_color_manual(
      values = c("FALSE" = "gray50", "TRUE" = "steelblue"),
      name = ifelse(use_adjusted_significance && "adj_significant" %in% colnames(results),
                    "Significant (Adjusted)", "Significant"),
      labels = c("FALSE" = "No", "TRUE" = "Yes")
    )
  }
  
  return(p)
}

# Example usage:
# # Create sample data with pre-calculated normalized ROC values
# set.seed(123)
# data <- data.frame(
#   Gene_name = rep(c("Gene1", "Gene2", "Gene3", "Gene4"), each = 50),
#   norm_roc = c(rnorm(50, 0.3, 0.1), 
#               rnorm(50, -0.1, 0.2), 
#               rnorm(50, 0.15, 0.15),
#               rnorm(50, 0.05, 0.1))
# )
# 
# # Test a single gene
# result_gene1 <- bootstrap_norm_roc_grouped(
#   data = data,
#   norm_col = "norm_roc",
#   group_col = "Gene_name",
#   group_value = "Gene1"
# )
# print(result_gene1)
# plot1 <- plot_bootstrap_dist(result_gene1)
# print(plot1)
# 
# # Test all genes
# results_all <- bootstrap_multiple_groups(
#   data = data,
#   norm_col = "norm_roc",
#   group_col = "Gene_name"
# )
# print(results_all)
# 
# # Create forest plot
# plot2 <- plot_forest(results_all)
# print(plot2)

# Remove a specific item from legend
# If you need to remove an item from a ggplot2 legend (such as color), 
# you can use the following approach after creating your plot:

# Example:
# p <- plot_forest(results_all, use_adjusted_significance = TRUE)
# 
# # To remove the color legend completely:
# p <- p + ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "none")
# 
# # To keep the legend but remove specific guides:
# p <- p + ggplot2::guides(color = "none")
# 
# # To create a table of results
# library(knitr)
# results_table <- results_all[, c("group", "mean", "ci_lower", "ci_upper", 
#                                 "p_value", "adj_p_value", "adj_significant")]
# results_table <- results_table[order(results_table$adj_p_value), ]
# kable(results_table, digits = 4)
# 
# print(p)
```

This code runs the bootstrap confidence interval testing so we can select statisticallyt signfiicant isos
```{r Calculating bootstrap confidence intervals and creates confidence interval plot, echo=FALSE}
bootstrap_scores <- bootstrap_multiple_groups(data = combined_model_performance_all_posneg_relative %>% 
                                    dplyr::filter(Group == 'Isoform'), 
                          norm_col = "Ceiling_normalised_ROC", 
                          group_col = 'Gene_name_postrans',
                          conf_level = 0.99,
                          n_bootstrap = 2000,
                          one_sided = TRUE,
                          adjust_method = "BH") 

#Note, p-values are zero or 1 in most cases - anyhow, we can just indicate significance, I guess.

#Could include this plot as supplementary (?)
```


```{r Loading variable importance classifier data, echo = FALSE}
#Variable importance
combined_variable_importance_set_1A <- bind_rows(mget(ls(pattern = 'iso_variable_contrast_importance_SET_1A_PAIR')))
rm(list = ls(pattern = 'iso_variable_contrast_importance_SET_1A_PAIR'))
#B
combined_variable_importance_set_B <- bind_rows(mget(ls(pattern = 'iso_variable_contrast_importance_SET_B_PAIR')))
rm(list = ls(pattern = 'iso_variable_contrast_importance_SET_B_PAIR'))
#C
combined_variable_importance_set_C <- bind_rows(mget(ls(pattern = 'iso_variable_contrast_importance_SET_C_PAIR')))
rm(list = ls(pattern = 'iso_variable_contrast_importance_SET_C_PAIR'))
#D
combined_variable_importance_set_D <- bind_rows(mget(ls(pattern = 'iso_variable_contrast_importance_SET_D_PAIR')))
rm(list = ls(pattern = 'iso_variable_contrast_importance_SET_D_PAIR'))
#
combined_variable_importance_all <- bind_rows(mget(ls(pattern = 'combined_variable_importance_set_'))) %>%
  left_join(x = ., y = gene_ids_match, by = join_by('SET' == 'SET', 'ISOFORM_PAIR' == 'ISOFORM_PAIR')) 

#
combined_overall <- combined_variable_importance_all %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  group_by(Gene_name, TRANSFECTION_STATUS) %>% 
  summarize('Combined_overall' = sum(Overall_avg),
            'Combined_overall_neg' = sum(Overall_avg_neg))%>% 
  dplyr::arrange(Combined_overall)


combined_variable_importance_all_w_overall <- combined_variable_importance_all %>% 
  left_join(., x = combined_overall, by = join_by('Gene_name' == 'Gene_name', 'TRANSFECTION_STATUS' == 'TRANSFECTION_STATUS')) %>%
  dplyr::mutate('contribution_prop' = (Overall_avg/Combined_overall)*100,
                'contribution_prop_sd' = (Overall_sd/Combined_overall)*100,
                'contribution_prop_neg' = (Overall_avg_neg/Combined_overall_neg)*100,
                'contribution_prop_neg_sd' = (Overall_sd_neg/Combined_overall_neg)*100)

# Combining the variable importance data for negtrans and postrans in a wider format
# Positive transfectants
combined_variable_importance_all_w_overall_postrans <- combined_variable_importance_all_w_overall %>% 
  dplyr::filter(TRANSFECTION_STATUS == 'POSITIVE') %>% 
  dplyr::select(SET, ISOFORM_PAIR, Gene_name, microexon, Exon_length_NT, EVENT_ID_vastDB, TRANSFECTION_STATUS, Feature,  Rank_median, contribution_prop, contribution_prop_sd,
                'Rank_median_negcontrol' = Rank_median_neg, 'contribution_prop_negcontrol' = contribution_prop_neg,
                'contribution_prop_negcontrol_sd' = contribution_prop_neg_sd)
# Negative transfectants
combined_variable_importance_all_w_overall_negtrans <- combined_variable_importance_all_w_overall %>% 
  dplyr::filter(TRANSFECTION_STATUS == 'NEGATIVE') %>% 
  dplyr::select(SET, ISOFORM_PAIR, Gene_name, microexon, Exon_length_NT, EVENT_ID_vastDB, TRANSFECTION_STATUS, Feature,  Rank_median, contribution_prop, contribution_prop_sd,
                'Rank_median_negcontrol' = Rank_median_neg, 'contribution_prop_negcontrol' = contribution_prop_neg,
                'contribution_prop_negcontrol_sd' = contribution_prop_neg_sd)

# Combined positive and negative transfectants
combined_variable_importance_all_w_overall_posneg <- combined_variable_importance_all_w_overall_postrans %>% 
  # Joining variable importance for postrans and negtrans
  dplyr::left_join(x = ., y = combined_variable_importance_all_w_overall_negtrans, by = join_by(SET, ISOFORM_PAIR, Gene_name, microexon, Exon_length_NT, EVENT_ID_vastDB, Feature), suffix = c('_pos', '_neg')) %>% 
  # Introducing the contribution proportion ratio (postrans/negtrans)
  dplyr::mutate(contribution_prop_ratio = contribution_prop_pos/contribution_prop_neg,
                contribution_prop_ratio_negcontrol = contribution_prop_negcontrol_pos/contribution_prop_negcontrol_neg,
                ratio_times_importance = contribution_prop_ratio * contribution_prop_pos,
                noteworthy_feature = if_else(ratio_times_importance >= ratio_times_importance_threshold & contribution_prop_ratio > ratio_threshold, TRUE, FALSE)) %>% 
  # Add p-values from the bootstrapping confidence intervals
    left_join(x = .,
            y = bootstrap_scores %>% dplyr::select(group, adj_p_value, adj_significant),
            by = join_by('Gene_name' == 'group'))

```

```{r xy plot of ratio x contribution prop versus contribution prop positive, echo =FALSE}
#Importance plot for controls 
combined_variable_importance_all_w_overall_posneg %>% 
  dplyr::filter(Gene_name %in% c('NegControl', 'PosControl')) %>% 
    ggplot(aes(x = contribution_prop_ratio, y = contribution_prop_ratio * contribution_prop_pos)) +
    geom_segment(aes(x = ratio_threshold, y = ratio_times_importance_threshold, 
                     xend = Inf, yend = ratio_times_importance_threshold), color = 'red', linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_segment(aes(x = ratio_threshold, y = ratio_times_importance_threshold, 
                     xend = ratio_threshold, yend = Inf), color = 'red', linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_point(aes(fill = noteworthy_feature), shape = 21, size = 5, color = 'black', alpha = 0.8)+
    scale_y_continuous(transform = 'log2',
                       limits = c(0.03125,32)) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,7))+
    xlab('Contribution ratio') +
    labs(fill = 'Noteworthy\nfeature') +
    ylab('Feature contribution (%)\nx\nContribution ratio') +
    ggtitle(paste('Identifying "Noteworthy" features')) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 16)+
  facet_wrap(~Gene_name)

```

```{r Function for generating importance plots for single isoforms, echo=FALSE}
generate_gene_plots <- function(IMPORTANCE_DATA, 
                                CELL_DATA, 
                                gene_name,
                                ratio_threshold = 1, 
                                ratio_times_importance_threshold = 1,
                                n = 10) {
  
  library(dplyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(purrr)
  # Create empty list to store plots
  plot_list <- list()
  
  # Filter data for the specified gene
  gene_data <- IMPORTANCE_DATA %>% 
    dplyr::filter(Gene_name == gene_name)
  
  # 1. Generate the main importance plot
  main_plot <- gene_data %>% 
    ggplot(aes(x = contribution_prop_ratio, y = contribution_prop_ratio * contribution_prop_pos)) +
    geom_segment(aes(x = ratio_threshold, y = ratio_times_importance_threshold, 
                     xend = Inf, yend = ratio_times_importance_threshold), color = 'red', linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_segment(aes(x = ratio_threshold, y = ratio_times_importance_threshold, 
                     xend = ratio_threshold, yend = Inf), color = 'red', linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_point(aes(fill = noteworthy_feature), shape = 21, size = 4, color = 'black', alpha = 0.8)+
    scale_y_continuous(transform = 'log2',
                       limits = c(0.03125,32)) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,7))+
    xlab('Contribution ratio') +
    labs(fill = 'Noteworthy\nfeature') +
    ylab('Feature contribution (%)\nx\nContribution ratio') +
    ggtitle(paste('"Noteworthy" features for', gene_name)) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 16) +
    facet_wrap(~Gene_name)
  
  # Add to plot list
  plot_list$main_importance_plot <- main_plot
  
  # 2. Generate bar plot of top n features with highest ratio_times_importance
  # Filter for noteworthy features and sort by ratio_times_importance
  top_features_data <- gene_data %>%
    dplyr::filter(noteworthy_feature == TRUE) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(ratio_times_importance = contribution_prop_ratio * contribution_prop_pos) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(ratio_times_importance)) %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = n)
  
  # Create the bar plot
  if (nrow(top_features_data) > 0) {
    feature_bar_plot <- top_features_data %>%
      ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Feature, ratio_times_importance), y = ratio_times_importance)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
      geom_hline(yintercept = ratio_times_importance_threshold, color = 'red', linewidth = 0.8)+
      scale_y_continuous(transform = 'log2',
                       limits = c(1,32)
                         ) +
      #coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1,32))+
      coord_flip() +
      #     coord_cartesian(ylim = c(1,32))+
      xlab("Feature") +
      ylab("Feature contribution (%) x Contribution ratio") +
      ggtitle(paste("Top", n, "'Noteworthy' features for", gene_name)) +
      theme_bw(base_size = 16)
    
    plot_list$top_features_bar_plot <- feature_bar_plot
  } else {
    # Add empty plot with message if no noteworthy features
    plot_list$top_features_bar_plot <- ggplot() + 
      annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 0.5, label = "No noteworthy features found") +
      theme_bw(base_size = 16)
  }
  
  # 3. Generate dot plots for top features vs MCHERRY intensity
  # Filter CELL_DATA for the specific gene
  cell_gene_data <- CELL_DATA %>%
    dplyr::mutate('Gene_name' = case_when(
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_1_PAIR_01' ~ 'ANK2',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_1_PAIR_03' ~ 'CLASP1_noEx_vs_ExA',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_1_PAIR_04' ~ 'CLASP1_ExB_vs_ExA+ExB',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_1_PAIR_08' ~ 'NUMB',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_1_PAIR_09' ~ 'SNX2',
      #
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_A_PAIR_01' ~ 'ANK2',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_A_PAIR_03' ~ 'CLASP1_noEx_vs_ExA',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_A_PAIR_04' ~ 'CLASP1_ExB_vs_ExA+ExB',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_A_PAIR_08' ~ 'NUMB',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_A_PAIR_09' ~ 'SNX2',
      #
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_1A_PAIR_01' ~ 'ANK2',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_1A_PAIR_02' ~ 'Apbb1',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_1A_PAIR_03' ~ 'CLASP1_noEx_vs_ExA', #
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_1A_PAIR_03bothEx' ~ 'CLASP1_noEx_vs_ExA+ExB',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_1A_PAIR_03DExVSPanEx' ~ 'CLASP1_noEx_vs_ExB',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_1A_PAIR_03Ex1VsBoth' ~ 'CLASP1_ExA_vs_ExA+ExB',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_1A_PAIR_03ExVsEx' ~ 'CLASP1_ExA_vs_ExB',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_1A_PAIR_04' ~ 'CLASP1_ExB_vs_ExA+ExB',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_1A_PAIR_05' ~ 'DCTN1',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_1A_PAIR_06' ~ 'VAV2',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_1A_PAIR_07' ~ 'FBXW11',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_1A_PAIR_08' ~ 'NUMB',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_1A_PAIR_09' ~ 'SNX2',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_1A_PAIR_10' ~ 'SPAG9',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_1A_PAIR_RAB5A' ~ 'NegControl',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_1A_PAIR_PosControl' ~ 'PosControl',
      #
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_B_PAIR_01' ~ 'AP1G1',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_B_PAIR_02' ~ 'DAAM1',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_B_PAIR_03' ~ 'DNM1L',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_B_PAIR_04' ~ 'DYNC1LI2',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_B_PAIR_05' ~ 'EEA1',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_B_PAIR_06' ~ 'KIF3A',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_B_PAIR_07' ~ 'ROBO1',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_B_PAIR_08' ~ 'SCYL2',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_B_PAIR_09' ~ 'SRC',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_B_PAIR_10' ~ 'TNK2',
      #
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_C_PAIR_02' ~ 'CLTA',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_C_PAIR_04' ~ 'DNM2',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_C_PAIR_05' ~ 'Dnm2',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_C_PAIR_06' ~ 'Dnm3',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_C_PAIR_07' ~ 'OCRL',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_C_PAIR_08' ~ 'PACSIN2',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_C_PAIR_09' ~ 'SH3GLB1',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_C_PAIR_10' ~ 'SH3KBP1',
      #
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_D_PAIR_01dex57VS168' ~ '*APP_noEx_vs_ExA', #Ex168nt is ExA, Ex57nt is ExB
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_D_PAIR_01wtVS168' ~ '*APP_noEx_vs_ExA+ExB',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_D_PAIR_01wtVS57' ~ '*APP_ExA_vs_ExA+ExB',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_D_PAIR_02' ~ '*ATXN2',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_D_PAIR_03' ~ '*EVI5',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_D_PAIR_04' ~ '*OCRL_D/E',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_D_PAIR_05' ~ '*PPFIA1',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_D_PAIR_06' ~ '*SH3GLB1_D/E',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_D_PAIR_07' ~ '*SH3KBP1_D/E',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_D_PAIR_08' ~ '*VAV2_noEx_vs_ExA', #ExA (is likely the downstream micro-exon), ExB is upstream micro-exon (I think)
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_D_PAIR_08bothEx' ~ '*VAV2_noEx_vs_ExA+ExB',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_D_PAIR_08DExVSaltEx' ~ '*VAV2_noEx_vs_ExB',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_D_PAIR_08Ex1VsBoth' ~ '*VAV2_ExA_vs_ExA+ExB',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_D_PAIR_08ExVsEx' ~ '*VAV2_ExA_vs_ExB',
      SET_PAIR == 'SET_D_PAIR_09' ~ '*VAV2_ExB_vs_ExA+ExB'
    )) %>% 
    dplyr::filter(Gene_name == gene_name) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(Rep_number = as.character(gsub('REP_',"",Experiment_Rep)))
  
  # Get the top features
  top_features <- top_features_data$Feature
  
  # Generate a dot plot for each top feature
  if (length(top_features) > 0) {
    feature_dot_plots <- map(top_features, function(feature_name) {
      # Get the ranking of this feature
      feature_rank <- which(top_features == feature_name)
      
      # Check if the feature exists in CELL_DATA
      if (feature_name %in% colnames(cell_gene_data)) {
        dot_plot <- cell_gene_data %>%
          ggplot(aes_string(y = feature_name, x = "Intensity_MeanIntensity_MCHERRY_BACKCORR",
                            color = "ISOFORM")) +
          geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 3, aes(shape = Rep_number)) +
          geom_smooth(aes(color = ISOFORM), method = "lm", se = TRUE, linewidth = 1.2, fill = 'black',alpha = 0.8)+
          scale_x_log10() +
          scale_shape_discrete(name = "Experimental\nRepeat") +
          ylab(paste('"Noteworthy" ',feature_rank)) +
          xlab("mCherry intensity (a.u.)") +
          ggtitle(paste("No.", feature_rank, "'Noteworthy' feature for", gene_name), 
                  subtitle = paste("Feature:", feature_name)) +
          theme_bw(base_size = 16)
      } else {
        # Create an empty plot with message if feature doesn't exist in CELL_DATA
        dot_plot <- ggplot() + 
          annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 0.5, 
                   label = paste("Feature", feature_name, "not found in CELL_DATA")) +
          theme_bw(base_size = 16) +
          ggtitle(paste("No.", feature_rank, "feature for", gene_name), 
                  subtitle = paste("Feature:", feature_name))
      }
      
      return(dot_plot)
    })
    
    # Add feature dot plots to plot list
    plot_list$feature_dot_plots <- feature_dot_plots
  } else {
    plot_list$feature_dot_plots <- list(
      ggplot() + 
        annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 0.5, label = "No features to plot") +
        theme_bw(base_size = 16)
    )
  }
  
  return(plot_list)
}

# Example usage:
# plot_list <- generate_gene_plots(
#   IMPORTANCE_DATA = combined_variable_importance_all_w_overall_posneg,
#   CELL_DATA = cell_data,
#   gene_name = "SNX2",
#   ratio_threshold = 1,
#   ratio_times_importance_threshold = 1,
#   n = 5
# )
# 
# # Accessing individual plots
# plot_list$main_importance_plot
# plot_list$top_features_bar_plot
# plot_list$feature_dot_plots[[1]]  # First feature dot plot
```

## Identifying top features for single isoform pairs

### Loading in single cell measurements

```{r Functions to load in single cell data, echo = FALSE}

#Function for loading in TRANSFECTED CELLS and SUMMARY datasets for each SET.
Load_transfected_cells_and_summary <- function(SET){
  setwd(paste0('//srv/scratch/weatheritt/pethan/HeLa_isoform_screen/ALL_CP_MEASUREMENTS_AND_FILTERED_TRANSFECTED_DATASETS'))
  #setwd(paste0('C:/Users/pethan/OneDrive - garvan.org.au/Documents/Australia_phd/Data/Isoform_imaging_screen_HeLa/ALL_CP_MEASUREMENTS_AND_FILTERED_TRANSFECTED_DATASETS'))
  
  Transfected_cell_files <- list.files() %>% 
    str_subset(pattern = '_TRANSFECTED_CELLS')
  Transfection_summary_files <- list.files() %>% 
    str_subset(pattern = '_TRANSFECTION_SUMMARY')
  
  load(paste0(str_subset(Transfected_cell_files, pattern = paste0(SET,'_REP_1_TRANSFECTED_CELLS'))))
  TRANSFECTED_CELLS_REP_1 <- Transfected_cellbodies
  rm(Transfected_cellbodies)
  
  load(paste0(str_subset(Transfected_cell_files, pattern = paste0(SET,'_REP_2_TRANSFECTED_CELLS'))))
  TRANSFECTED_CELLS_REP_2 <- Transfected_cellbodies
  rm(Transfected_cellbodies)
  
  load(paste0(str_subset(Transfected_cell_files, pattern = paste0(SET,'_REP_3_TRANSFECTED_CELLS'))))
  TRANSFECTED_CELLS_REP_3 <- Transfected_cellbodies
  rm(Transfected_cellbodies)
  
  Transfected_cells <- bind_rows(TRANSFECTED_CELLS_REP_1,
                                 TRANSFECTED_CELLS_REP_2,
                                 TRANSFECTED_CELLS_REP_3)
  
  load(paste0(str_subset(Transfection_summary_files, pattern = paste0(SET,'_REP_1_TRANSFECTION_SUMMARY'))))
  TRANSFECTION_SUMMARY_REP_1 <- Transfection_summary
  rm(Transfection_summary)
  
  load(paste0(str_subset(Transfection_summary_files, pattern = paste0(SET,'_REP_2_TRANSFECTION_SUMMARY'))))
  TRANSFECTION_SUMMARY_REP_2 <- Transfection_summary
  rm(Transfection_summary)
  
  load(paste0(str_subset(Transfection_summary_files, pattern = paste0(SET,'_REP_3_TRANSFECTION_SUMMARY'))))
  TRANSFECTION_SUMMARY_REP_3 <- Transfection_summary
  rm(Transfection_summary)
  
  Transfection_summary <- bind_rows(TRANSFECTION_SUMMARY_REP_1,
                                    TRANSFECTION_SUMMARY_REP_2,
                                    TRANSFECTION_SUMMARY_REP_3)
  return(list(Transfected_cells, Transfection_summary))
}
#Function for collected summary of REPEAT summaries
COLLECTED_SUMMARY <- function(DATASET){
  coll_summ <- DATASET %>%  
    ungroup() %>% 
    group_by(Experiment_Set, Metadata_Well) %>% 
    summarise(Transfected = sum(Transfected_TRUE),
              n_cells = sum(n_cells),
              Transfection_rate = round(Transfected/n_cells * 100, digits = 1)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate('Transfected_REP_1' = DATASET$Transfected_TRUE[DATASET$Experiment_Rep == 'REP_1'],
                  'Transfected_REP_2' = DATASET$Transfected_TRUE[DATASET$Experiment_Rep == 'REP_2'],
                  'Transfected_REP_3' = DATASET$Transfected_TRUE[DATASET$Experiment_Rep == 'REP_3'],
                  'n_cells_REP_1' = DATASET$n_cells[DATASET$Experiment_Rep == 'REP_1'],
                  'n_cells_REP_2' = DATASET$n_cells[DATASET$Experiment_Rep == 'REP_2'],
                  'n_cells_REP_3' = DATASET$n_cells[DATASET$Experiment_Rep == 'REP_3'],
                  'Transfection_rate_REP_1' = round(Transfected_REP_1/n_cells_REP_1 * 100, digits = 1),
                  'Transfection_rate_REP_2' = round(Transfected_REP_2/n_cells_REP_2 * 100, digits = 1),
                  'Transfection_rate_REP_3' = round(Transfected_REP_3/n_cells_REP_3 * 100, digits = 1))
  
  #coll_summ %>% 
  #  ungroup() %>% 
  #  group_by(Experiment_Set) %>% 
  #
  return(coll_summ)
}

remove_non_mcherry_vars <- function(TRANSFECTED_SET, ONLY_TRANSFECTED = FALSE, REDUCE_FEATURES = TRUE, ONLY_NEGTRANSFECTED = FALSE){
  if(REDUCE_FEATURES == TRUE){
    Reduced_dataset <- TRANSFECTED_SET %>% 
      dplyr::select(ImageNumber, ObjectNumber, OBJECT_ID, Metadata_Well, CONTROL,Experiment_Set, Experiment_Rep, SET_PAIR, ISOFORM,TRANSFECTED, 
                    starts_with('Intensity_') & ends_with('_MCHERRY_BACKCORR'),
                    starts_with('Correlation_'),
                    starts_with('Granularity_'), starts_with('RadialDistribution'), starts_with('Texture_'), starts_with('AreaShape_')) %>% 
      dplyr::select(!c(starts_with('Correlation') & contains('GOLGIN97_AUTOF_CORR'))) %>% 
      na.omit()
  }
  if(REDUCE_FEATURES == FALSE){
    Reduced_dataset <- TRANSFECTED_SET %>% 
      dplyr::select(ImageNumber, ObjectNumber, OBJECT_ID, Metadata_Well, CONTROL,Experiment_Set, Experiment_Rep, SET_PAIR, ISOFORM,TRANSFECTED, everything()) %>% 
      dplyr::select(!c(Experiment_SetRep, ends_with('_BACKGROUND'), Number_Object_Number, Parent_Segmentation)) %>% 
      na.omit()
  }
  if(ONLY_TRANSFECTED == TRUE){
    Reduced_dataset <- Reduced_dataset %>% 
      dplyr::filter(TRANSFECTED == TRUE)
  }
  if(ONLY_NEGTRANSFECTED == TRUE){
    Reduced_dataset <- Reduced_dataset %>% 
      dplyr::filter(TRANSFECTED == FALSE)
  }
  return(Reduced_dataset)
}
#
alternative_comparisons_set_1A <- function(DATASET, SET_NAME){
  alt_pairClasp_altEx <- DATASET %>% 
    dplyr::filter(SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_03') & ISOFORM == 'DEx' | SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_04') & ISOFORM == 'DEx') %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(
      ISOFORM = case_when(SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_03') & ISOFORM == 'DEx' ~ 'DEx',
                          SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_04') & ISOFORM == 'DEx' ~ 'PanEx'),
      SET_PAIR = paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_03DExVSPanEx'),
      SET_PAIR_REP_ISOFORM = paste0(SET_PAIR,'_',Experiment_Rep,'_',ISOFORM))
  
  alt_pairClasp_bothex <- DATASET %>% 
    dplyr::filter(SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_03') & ISOFORM == 'DEx' | SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_04') & ISOFORM == 'WT') %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(ISOFORM = case_when(SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_03') & ISOFORM == 'DEx' ~ 'DEx',
                                      SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_04') & ISOFORM == 'WT' ~ 'Both'),
                  SET_PAIR = paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_03bothEx'),
                  SET_PAIR_REP_ISOFORM = paste0(SET_PAIR,'_',Experiment_Rep,'_',ISOFORM))
  
  alt_pairClasp_exvsex <- DATASET %>% 
    dplyr::filter(SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_03') & ISOFORM == 'WT' | SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_04') & ISOFORM == 'DEx') %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(
      ISOFORM = case_when(SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_03') & ISOFORM == 'WT' ~ 'Ex1',
                          SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_04') & ISOFORM == 'DEx' ~ 'PanEx'),
      SET_PAIR = paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_03ExVsEx'),
      SET_PAIR_REP_ISOFORM = paste0(SET_PAIR,'_',Experiment_Rep,'_',ISOFORM))
  
  alt_pairClasp_ex1vsboth <- DATASET %>% 
    dplyr::filter(SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_03') & ISOFORM == 'WT' | SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_04') & ISOFORM == 'WT') %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(
      ISOFORM = case_when(SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_03') & ISOFORM == 'WT' ~ 'Ex1',
                          SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_04') & ISOFORM == 'WT' ~ 'Both'),
      SET_PAIR = paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_03Ex1VsBoth'),
      SET_PAIR_REP_ISOFORM = paste0(SET_PAIR,'_',Experiment_Rep,'_',ISOFORM))
  
  Transfected_only_without_outliers_1A_ALTERNATIVE_COMPARISONS <- DATASET %>% 
    dplyr::filter(SET_PAIR %in% c('SET_1_PAIR_MCHERRY','SET_1_PAIR_RAB5A', 'SET_A_PAIR_MCHERRY','SET_A_PAIR_RAB5A','SET_1A_PAIR_MCHERRY','SET_1A_PAIR_RAB5A')) %>% 
    bind_rows(., alt_pairClasp_altEx, alt_pairClasp_bothex, alt_pairClasp_exvsex,alt_pairClasp_ex1vsboth) %>% 
    na.omit()
  
  return(Transfected_only_without_outliers_1A_ALTERNATIVE_COMPARISONS)
}

#
alternative_comparisons_set_D <- function(DATASET, SET_NAME){ # DATASET is the transfected cells, SET_NAME is either SET_D or SET_E (Other sets don't have specific pairs)
  alt_pair01_wt57 <- DATASET %>% 
    dplyr::filter(SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_01') & ISOFORM %in% c('WT', 'DEx57nt')) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(
      ISOFORM = case_when(ISOFORM == 'WT' ~ 'WT',
                          ISOFORM == 'DEx57nt' ~ 'DEx'),
      SET_PAIR = paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_01wtVS57'),
      SET_PAIR_REP_ISOFORM = paste0(SET_PAIR,'_',Experiment_Rep,'_',ISOFORM))
  
  alt_pair01_wt168 <- DATASET %>% 
    dplyr::filter(SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_01') & ISOFORM %in% c('WT', 'DEx168nt')) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(
      ISOFORM = case_when(ISOFORM == 'WT' ~ 'WT',
                          ISOFORM == 'DEx168nt' ~ 'DEx'),
      SET_PAIR = paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_01wtVS168'),
      SET_PAIR_REP_ISOFORM = paste0(SET_PAIR,'_',Experiment_Rep,'_',ISOFORM))
  
  alt_pair01_dexdex <- DATASET %>% 
    dplyr::filter(SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_01') & ISOFORM %in% c('DEx57nt', 'DEx168nt')) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(
      ISOFORM = case_when(ISOFORM == 'DEx57nt' ~ 'DEx57nt',
                          ISOFORM == 'DEx168nt' ~ 'DEx168nt'),
      SET_PAIR = paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_01dex57VS168'),
      SET_PAIR_REP_ISOFORM = paste0(SET_PAIR,'_',Experiment_Rep,'_',ISOFORM))
  #
  alt_pair08altex <- DATASET %>% 
    dplyr::filter(SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_08') & ISOFORM == 'DEx' | SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_09') & ISOFORM == 'DEx') %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(
      ISOFORM = case_when(SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_08') & ISOFORM == 'DEx' ~ 'DEx',
                          SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_09') & ISOFORM == 'DEx' ~ 'AltEx'),
      SET_PAIR = paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_08DExVSaltEx'),
      SET_PAIR_REP_ISOFORM = paste0(SET_PAIR,'_',Experiment_Rep,'_',ISOFORM))
  
  
  alt_pair08bothex <- DATASET %>% 
    dplyr::filter(SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_08') & ISOFORM == 'DEx' | SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_09') & ISOFORM == 'WT') %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(ISOFORM = case_when(SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_08') & ISOFORM == 'DEx' ~ 'DEx',
                                      SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_09') & ISOFORM == 'WT' ~ 'Both'),
                  SET_PAIR = paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_08bothEx'),
                  SET_PAIR_REP_ISOFORM = paste0(SET_PAIR,'_',Experiment_Rep,'_',ISOFORM))
  
  alt_pair08exvsex <- DATASET %>% 
    dplyr::filter(SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_08') & ISOFORM == 'WT' | SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_09') & ISOFORM == 'DEx') %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(
      ISOFORM = case_when(SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_08') & ISOFORM == 'WT' ~ 'Ex1',
                          SET_PAIR == paste0(SET_NAME,'_PAIR_09') & ISOFORM == 'DEx' ~ 'Ex2'),
```
